object_identification.name: blue pen
object_identification.attributes: slim cylindrical blue plastic pen with a cap, lying on the wooden tabletop near the left-center foreground, slightly below the blue beverage can and left of the USB stick
object_identification.position_in_scene: foreground on the desk, slightly left of center

grasp.contact_zone: mid-body of the pen near the barrel, avoiding the tip and cap end
grasp.accessibility: high; the pen is isolated on the tabletop with clear approach space from above and from the side

planning.approach: move the gripper above the pen with a collision-free vertical approach, then descend slowly while monitoring the nearby USB stick and cable on the right
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: align the gripper fingers parallel to the pen axis and center the jaws over the pen’s cylindrical body
planning.grasp_contact: close the fingers gently on the pen barrel with just enough force to prevent slip, keeping contact away from the tapered tip and cap edge
planning.lift_stabilization: lift vertically a short distance, then hold steady with low grip force and verify the pen remains aligned without rotation

use.description: write or annotate on paper or a screen input surface if applicable
use.link_to_grasp: the precision grasp enables controlled tip orientation for writing
use.physical_constraints: requires the pen tip to remain unobstructed and the grip to allow fine rotational control

use.description: place the pen into a holder or hand it to a person
use.link_to_grasp: a stable mid-body grasp supports safe transport and placement
use.physical_constraints: must avoid dropping or pressing too hard, and keep the tip from contacting other objects

robustness.assumptions: the pen is rigid, lightweight, and not stuck to the table; the gripper can fit around its diameter
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if the exact pose is uncertain, use visual servoing to re-center over the pen before closure and reduce grip force if slippage or misalignment is detected

object_identification.name: blue pen

planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper fingers aligned parallel to the long axis of the pen
planning.grasp_contact: light pinch on the barrel to reduce contact area and prevent slippage
planning.lift_stabilization: vertical lift with slight clamping force to counteract torque caused by the pen center of mass

use.description: writing on paper
use.link_to_grasp: none required for writing, but relevant for transport

use.description: holding as a pointer device

use.physical_constraints: needing friction to prevent slipping at the back

robustness.assumptions: pen is rigid enough not to bend and lies flat on the surface
robustness.uncertainty_handling: use impedance control during lift to handle potential slight tilting or lack of friction

object_identification.name: blue pen

grasp.contact_zone: mid-section and tip
grasp.accessibility: partially accessible from above and side angles, no significant occlusions

planning.approach: vertical descent from above at a slight angle towards the pen’s mid-section
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: end-effector (e.g., gripper fingers) oriented parallel to the pen’s axis, fingers slightly open

planning.lift_stabilization: lift vertically upwards with slight rotation to align with the robot’s end-effector axis, maintaining grip pressure

use.description: writing or drawing on paper, digital notetaking devices, or whiteboards

use.physical_constraints: tip must be clean and unobstructed; sufficient grip to prevent slipping during use

use.description: highlighting or pointing during presentations, pointing out specific areas on screens or documents

object_identification.name: blue retractable ballpoint pen
object_identification.attributes: retractable click-style blue plastic body, smooth cylindrical shaft, attached clip, exposed pointed writing tip, uniform blue coloration
object_identification.position_in_scene: rests on the light wooden foreground table, positioned between a grey eraser to its left, a white USB drive to its right, below the blue energy drink can, and above the deflated orange balloon

grasp.contact_zone: the mid-section of the pen's cylindrical shaft, avoiding the top click mechanism and bottom writing tip
grasp.accessibility: fully unobstructed, sits flat on the table with no objects blocking access, reachable from all angles above the table surface

planning.approach: robotic arm approaches the pen from directly above the table, moving vertically down to align gripper fingers with the pen's shaft
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper aligned parallel to the pen's long axis, positioning the two pinch fingers to evenly wrap around the shaft's circumference
planning.grasp_contact: gripper closes to apply moderate, uniform pressure on the pen's mid-shaft to prevent slipping or rotation
planning.lift_stabilization: after grasping, lift the pen 15cm vertically off the table first to confirm stable hold, then move to the target location while keeping the pen's long axis horizontal to maintain balance

use.description: write handwritten notes or mark up physical paper documents
use.link_to_grasp: retains the precision pinch grasp, with the robot's wrist adjusting to angle the pen's writing tip to a 45-degree angle against the paper, applying consistent downward pressure to produce ink marks
use.physical_constraints: requires a smooth flat paper surface, sufficient ink remaining in the pen's cartridge, and wrist mobility to adjust the pen's angle for controlled writing

use.description: act as a presentation pointer to highlight content on meeting screens or wall displays
use.link_to_grasp: maintains the same precision pinch grasp, with the robotic arm extending to position the pen's tip toward the content being highlighted, adjusting arm position to move the pen across the display area as needed
use.physical_constraints: requires unobstructed line of access to the display, the pen's blue color provides sufficient contrast against most light-colored backgrounds to be visible to meeting attendees

robustness.assumptions: the pen is rigid and maintains its shape during grasping, the table surface is stable so the pen does not roll pre-grasp, the pen's shaft diameter fits within the gripper's pinch range
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if the pen shifts during initial approach, the robot's vision system detects the new position and re-aligns the gripper; if the grasp is unstable after lifting, the robot lowers the pen back to the table, adjusts the contact position on the shaft, and re-attempts the grasp

object_identification.name: grey mug

grasp.type: side pinch or power grasp on the handle

planning.lift_stabilization: lift vertically with the mug kept upright, then pause to verify stability and compensate for liquid slosh by keeping motions smooth

use.description: bring the mug to a person for drinking

robustness.assumptions: the mug is lightweight, graspable by the handle, and not glued or stuck to the table

use.description: Holding a hot beverage such as coffee or tea

object_identification.name: grey mug
object_identification.attributes: cylindrical body with a single C-shaped handle, solid grey color, appears to be made of ceramic
object_identification.position_in_scene: Resting upright on the wooden-grain table surface, in the lower right foreground, near the edge closest to the viewer.

grasp.contact_zone: The outer cylindrical surfaces of the mug's main body, with jaws opposed, avoiding the handle completely
grasp.accessibility: High; the object is fully separated from others, with ample clearance for robot fingers from above and the sides of the main body.

planning.approach: From directly above (vertical approach) with a slight, calculated offset towards the center of mass to account for the handle, using real-time spatial awareness to plan a collision-free path that maintains clearance from other objects.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the parallel gripper jaws so that they are equidistant from the mug's centerline, with the fingers oriented for a firm pinch on opposed sides.
planning.grasp_contact: Simultaneously actuate gripper fingers to move inwards until a predefined force threshold is reached, ensuring a firm, secure grip without damaging the ceramic material.
planning.lift_stabilization: Perform a gradual vertical lift while simultaneously applying an upward lift force and monitoring for any translational or rotational slip using high-accuracy force-torque sensors and visual feedback.

use.description: Lifting and moving the mug to a non-predefined location on the table.
use.link_to_grasp: The current grasp provides a solid, balanced hold that can withstand translational forces during a move operation.
use.physical_constraints: Path must be free of major obstacles (like the blue can) and avoid rapid decelerations that might induce sloshing within the liquid contents.

use.description: Simulating a drinking or pouring motion by rotating the mug along its top edge.
use.link_to_grasp: This grasp configuration allows for uninhibited rotation without risk of the hand getting in the way, though a re-grasp might be needed to apply the correct torque and prevent wrist joint limit issues.
use.physical_constraints: Mug should be lifted sufficiently high so the rotation doesn't cause contact with the table surface, and the acceleration/deceleration must be smooth and constant to keep fluid contained.

robustness.assumptions: Assumes the mug is primarily full of a moderately viscous fluid (not an extremely dense/heavy or highly explosive liquid), and the table surface provides sufficient friction prevent it from sliding pre-grasp.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Integrate data from on-arm camera (like a standard depth sensor) with tactical and torque sensors to provide real-time updates on mug position, surface compliance, and center of mass changes, allowing the robot to dynamically adapt its path and gripping force accordingly.

planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the parallel gripper jaws with the long axis of the flattened balloon to maximize contact area.

planning.lift_stabilization: Slight upward pull to confirm adherence or friction engagement, then lift. The object will hang and deform when lifted.

use.description: inflate the balloon
use.link_to_grasp: This grasp holds the item by its main body, leaving the nozzle/stem end free for alignment with an inflation nozzle.
use.physical_constraints: The material properties (elasticity, adhesion) are key. A standard rigid gripper may crush it or slip; a soft robot or vacuum gripper is superior.

use.description: place the balloon into a specific container
use.link_to_grasp: Maintain the grasp and translate/rotate the end effector to move the item to a storage bin, then release.
use.physical_constraints: Static electricity might cause the light, rubbery material to stick to the gripper surface even after release, requiring a non-stick coating or an active 'blow-off' mechanism.

robustness.assumptions: The object is relatively clean and dry. A simple friction-based model is assumed for the compliant gripper pads.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Use force tactile feedback to detect thin contacts. If slippage is detected, apply a gentle vertical force to use the table for support while tightening the grip slightly.

use.description: inflate the balloon to use as a lightweight decorative object for office desk or event decor

object_identification.name: blue can

grasp.contact_zone: The gripper should make contact with the cylindrical sides of the can, roughly at the mid-section for optimal balance.

planning.approach: From above, with a downward linear motion towards the center of the can't length.

use.description: Moving the can to a different location, for example, to clear the table or place it on a shelf.

use.physical_constraints: Path must be clear; the gripper can potentially block parts of the can, so placing it in a specific tight spot vertically (e.g., inside a narrow cup) might require a shift to a grasp from the top.

use.description: Dispensing the content of the can if it contains any, which would require an additional action to open it and then potentially re-gripping.
use.link_to_grasp: The current grasp is for transport; a different grasp (likely on the ends/tops) would be needed for a mechanism/sensor to open the tab and a different robotic gesture for pouring.
use.physical_constraints: Requires complex multi-action integration (transport, open, re-grip, pour) and additional context about whether the can is open and how to orient it during pouring.

robustness.assumptions: The can is sufficiently rigid to be grasped by a robotic hand without excessive deformation and does not contain sensitive contents that could spill with a standard parallel jaw grasp on the sides.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Implement a force-torque sensor feedback mechanism on the end-effector to detect the initial contact and adjust gripping force in real-time, preventing crush or slip.

object_identification.name: stirrer
object_identification.attributes: A small white disposable stick-like stirrer with a narrow rectangular profile and rounded ends, lying flat on the tabletop near the mug on the right side of the scene.
object_identification.position_in_scene: Lower right quadrant of the table, just left of the gray mug and above the orange balloon.

grasp.contact_zone: Along the middle section of the slender shaft, avoiding the ends to maximize control and keep the stirrer aligned.
grasp.accessibility: High accessibility because it is isolated on the open tabletop and not heavily occluded, though it is close to the mug and should be approached carefully.

planning.approach: Plan a top-down approach from above the stirrer with the gripper centered over the shaft and clear of the mug rim.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Orient the gripper fingers parallel to the long axis of the stirrer with a slightly open aperture sized for a thin flat object.
planning.grasp_contact: Close the fingers gently on the mid-shaft until both sides make stable contact without bending the plastic stick.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift slowly vertically while maintaining a light pinch force and keeping the object level to prevent slip or flexing.

use.description: Stir a beverage in the mug.
use.link_to_grasp: The precision pinch grasp provides enough control to insert the stirrer into the drink and rotate it.
use.physical_constraints: The stirrer is thin and flexible, so torque must be low and the motion should avoid excessive bending.

use.description: Mix a small sample or liquid in a container during an office or lab task.
use.link_to_grasp: The same controlled grasp allows the robot to place the stirrer accurately into narrow vessels and move it in a circular pattern.
use.physical_constraints: The object is lightweight and disposable, so the robot should avoid crushing it and should limit forces to prevent snapping.

robustness.assumptions: The stirrer is a lightweight rigid-or-semi-rigid disposable stick lying unobstructed on the table surface.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If pose estimation is uncertain, the robot should use a slow visual servoing approach, re-center on the visible shaft, and reduce grip force until stable contact is confirmed.

object_identification.name: stirrer
object_identification.attributes: wooden, small, flat, white, rectangular, thin
object_identification.position_in_scene: on the light-brown wooden table surface, to the left of the gray mug

grasp.contact_zone: near the center along the long edges
grasp.accessibility: high accessibility from the top, moderate accessibility from the sides

planning.approach: vertical approach with fingers open, aligning the fingers with the long central axis of the stirrer
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: end-effector aligned in a top-down or slight-angled downward pose, fingers positioned above the flat surfaces
planning.grasp_contact: gentle pinch closing from opposite side flat sides, making contact at two points for stability
planning.lift_stabilization: vertical lift with closed fingers, slight rotation to ensure the stirrer is clear of the table and mug before any lateral movement

use.description: stirring a hot drink
use.link_to_grasp: This tool is commonly used to stir liquids, in this case, likely coffee in the mug to dissolve sugar or other add-ins.
use.physical_constraints: needs an auxiliary object (the drink in the mug), and the motion must be circular inside the constraint of the mug, while being aware of the hot fluid

use.description: cleaning the rim of the mug or table surface
use.link_to_grasp: a secondary use could be as a small scraper, for wiping away a small spill from the cup's outer edge or a dry coffee particle from the table
use.physical_constraints: needs control of the end applied to a surface, with minimal pressure to avoid breaking or sliding rather than cleaning

robustness.assumptions: the object is rigid enough to withstand a pinch grip and has two flat, parallel sides suitable for a two-finger pinch-type gripper
robustness.uncertainty_handling: in case of a potential slippage detection from force feedback, slight increase in jaw force or slight upward movement

object_identification.name: pkeleton_stirrer
object_identification.attributes: long cylindrical shape, semi-rigid plastic or rubber material, flat triangular end for stirring, orange cup-like cap, approximate length ~25cm, lighter and flat grip section
object_identification.position_in_scene: lie on the right side of the table, partially under the dark grey mug, oriented horizontally with triangular end facing the top-left direction

grasp.contact_zone: grip section surrounding the narrow neck of the stirrer and the flat wider part just above the triangular end
grasp.accessibility: low to medium, visibility and slight obstruction from mug on top

planning.approach: slightly above and towards the middle of the flat wider section of the stirrer, approaching from a diagonally downward angle
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: end-effector oriented perpendicular to the stirrer’s long axis, fingers semi-opened and parallel to each other
planning.grasp_contact: index finger and thumb contact the stirrer’s neck roughly 3-4cm below the orange cup cap; middle finger optionally wraps underneath if additional stabilizing force is needed
planning.lift_stabilization: robotic arm applies a minimal upward and rightward force to lift while controlling rotational motion to prevent toppling

use.description: mixing liquids in a glass or cup especially when stirring ingredients like tea, coffee, or smoothie ingredients to ensure even blending
use.link_to_grasp: grasp section is close to the stirring tip for ease and precise control during mixing actions
use.physical_constraints: endurability to withstand repeated operations in moist or sticky environments; proper firmness to stir thorough without breaking

use.description: performing actions like aerating and frothing liquids such as milk for lattes or cappuccinos in a kitchen or bar setting
use.link_to_grasp: robust grip from original grasp location ensures rotations are stable and vantage point prevents collision between the stirrer and container sides
use.physical_constraints: Robust design ensuring gentle yet firm enough to aerate without pinching or spilling liquid

robustness.assumptions: stirrer surface is unclean, no liquids, predefined RGB color detection ranges accommodated for end-effector and depth data parallel light setup
robustness.uncertainty_handling: slip prevention via electrostatic Thumb/finger dressing; Utilizes tactile sensors for safe grip pressure and adaptive end-stopping to avoid pinch marks

object_identification.name: wooden stirrer (popsicle stick stirrer)
object_identification.attributes: slender flat wooden stick, smooth surface, white-tipped end, lightweight, single-use disposable design
object_identification.position_in_scene: lying horizontally on the foreground light wooden desk, between the wired black computer mouse and the gray coffee mug, to the right of the white USB flash drive, fully unobscured on the desk surface

grasp.contact_zone: the central midsection of the stirrer, avoiding both ends to prevent contact with food residue and avoid slippage
grasp.accessibility: fully accessible, no surrounding objects block the stirrer's midsection, with unobstructed approach from above the desk

planning.approach: move the robotic gripper horizontally from a position above the desk, directly towards the stirrer's midsection, aligned parallel to the stirrer's length to avoid misalignment
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: orient the gripper's two opposing pinch pads perpendicular to the stirrer's length, so they can evenly contact the flat top and bottom surfaces of the thin stick
planning.grasp_contact: close the gripper's fingers to apply a consistent low force, sufficient to hold the lightweight wood without bending or breaking it, on the top and bottom of the stirrer's midsection
planning.lift_stabilization: lift the grasped stirrer vertically 10cm above the desk surface, pause to confirm grip stability via force feedback, before initiating any movement to prevent collisions with nearby objects

use.description: stir the hot chocolate beverage inside the adjacent gray ceramic mug to evenly mix the drink's ingredients
use.link_to_grasp: retain the original precision pinch grasp on the stirrer's midsection, only rotate the end-effector to angle the free end of the stirrer to submerge into the mug's liquid, no re-grasp required
use.physical_constraints: limit mixing force to 5N to avoid snapping the thin wooden stirrer, keep the gripped midsection above the liquid line to prevent liquid contamination of the robotic gripper

use.description: act as a small disposable spatula to spread soft, low-viscosity substances like cream, frosting, or paste across a flat surface
use.link_to_grasp: maintain the same midsection pinch grasp, adjust the end-effector's angle to position the stirrer's flat end flush against the target surface for spreading, no grasp repositioning needed
use.physical_constraints: only use with substances weighing less than 100g to avoid bending or breaking the wooden stirrer, do not use on hard or high-friction surfaces that could cause the stirrer to splinter

robustness.assumptions: the stirrer is structurally intact with no cracks or damage, the desk surface is flat and stable, the stirrer remains stationary during the approach phase
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if initial grasp alignment is off, reposition the gripper by up to 1cm to correct contact with the midsection, use force feedback to confirm grip strength before lifting to prevent dropping the thin lightweight stick, and re-plan the trajectory if the stirrer shifts during approach to avoid pushing other desk objects
